response.justification: The candidate lacks substantial C++ experience in industrial or embedded contexts, with only a brief, outdated role at National Instruments involving LabWindows/C++ — insufficient to meet the core requirement of 8–12 years of C++ development for high-performance, secure, multi-threaded industrial automation systems. Their recent work is dominated by TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with no evidence of STL, message brokers, MQTT, IEC 62443, or industrial protocol expertise.
strengths: Strong full-stack and cross-platform development experience
Demonstrated leadership in open-source and community technical initiatives
Proven ability to deliver complex systems across diverse domains
concerns: Minimal recent or relevant C++ experience for the role’s core requirements
No demonstrated exposure to industrial automation protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, etc.)
Lacks evidence of SDL or IEC 62443 compliance, cloud security, or embedded systems design
